extra.message:
User input from 'name' query parameter is directly concatenated into HTML string without escaping, then rendered via Jinja2 Template. This allows Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). The name parameter (line 19) is included in HTML content (line 38) without sanitization: f"

Hello, {name}!

...". An attacker can inject JavaScript code via the name parameter.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: Hardcoded secret value 'John Ripper' for SUPER_SECRET_NAME directly in source code. This sensitive credential is exposed in version control and all distributed copies of the code. Should use environment variables instead (see commented FIXME).

Hardcoded secret token '5u93R53Cr3tT0k3n' for SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN directly in source code. This sensitive credential is exposed in version control and all distributed copies of the code. Should use environment variables instead (see commented FIXME).

External URL from config.PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL is used in requests.get() without validation. While the URL is from configuration, an attacker who can control environment variables or the .env file could direct requests to arbitrary servers (SSRF). Additionally, the response is used as trusted data without validation.
